model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size processes more unique examples per run and often improves early-loss stability; pairing it with a modest Adam LR increase (to the common 1e-3 regime) helps recover update magnitude and usually improves short-horizon convergence with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under fixed-step evaluation, increasing batch size further should expose the model to more unique training examples within the same 256 updates, which often improves early validation loss in this compact CNN regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After successful batch-size scaling to 256, a moderate LR increase should better match the larger-batch regime and improve optimization progress within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has improved consistently with LR increases, suggesting the run is still optimization-limited at 256 steps; a further moderate LR increase is a high-value continuation of the same successful direction.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The fixed-step cosine schedule degraded validation loss, so disabling it reverts to the better-performing constant-LR regime while preserving the successful batch-size/LR settings.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the current high-throughput setting (batch 256, lr 2e-3), AdamW can improve optimization/generalization balance versus classic Adam by decoupling weight decay, often helping validation loss in short-budget CNN training.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW hurt this workload at the current settings, so reverting to Adam restores the best-performing optimizer choice while keeping other gains intact.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Validation loss appears optimization-improved but may now be capacity-limited; a modest width increase is a simple architecture change that often yields better CIFAR-10 loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increasing width hurt validation loss under the fixed-step budget, so reverting to the previous channel size restores the stronger baseline configuration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Given the short fixed-step regime and no overfitting signal, removing weight decay can reduce underfitting pressure and improve achieved validation loss within the limited optimization budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Zero weight decay regressed, so restore it and test a lower Adam beta2 to make second-moment estimates adapt faster in this short 256-step regime, which can improve early optimization.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Lower beta2 regressed, so restore the better Adam moments and probe a small LR increase around the current best point to test whether optimization is still slightly step-limited.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The LR increase regressed, so revert to 2e-3 and probe a slightly larger batch to increase unique-example coverage per fixed 256 steps without moving too far from the current best configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size 320 regressed versus the known best configuration, so reverting to 256 restores the strongest validated setting before exploring further tweaks.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()","new":"            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"A small amount of gradient clipping can improve stability at the current aggressive LR/batch regime and sometimes yields better validation loss in short-budget training without changing core optimization settings.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            optimizer.step()","new":"            loss.backward()
            optimizer.step()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Gradient clipping regressed and is removed; then a small LR decrease from the current best point tests whether slightly less aggressive updates improve final validation loss at fixed 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.9e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.85e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"A small improvement came from lowering LR to 1.9e-3, so a finer local sweep downward is a high-probability next probe for additional fixed-budget loss gains.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.85e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The local LR sweep is trending positive as LR decreases (2e-3 -> 1.9e-3 -> 1.85e-3), so one more small downward step is the highest-yield remaining exploration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.85e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 1.8e-3 run regressed, so revert to 1.85e-3, which is the best validated learning-rate setting found in this trajectory.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
